data.title: Domestic drillers increasing AI grid power, no oil production reduction planned as demand steady till 2050.
data.description: Prairie Operating Co., in a recent Equity Insider News Commentary from Vancouver, BC on July 17, 2025, stated that there are no immediate intentions to cut oil production. OPEC forecasts indicate that oil demand will not reach its peak until 2050. This information suggests that despite various factors, the company plans to maintain its current level of oil production for the foreseeable future.

data.title: Galileo launches free agent reliability platform for multi-agent systems, combining observability, evaluation, and guardrails.
data.description: A trailblazing AI assessment firm has launched a groundbreaking platform in San Francisco on July 17, 2025. This platform uniquely integrates observability, evaluation, and guardrails targeting multi-agent systems. Galileo, renowned as the top AI reliability platform, has introduced this industry-first solution to provide comprehensive support for evaluations. The platform aims to enhance the efficacy and safety of multi-agent systems using advanced technology.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 and small caps hit new highs, S&P 500 at 6,300, Dow Jones at 44,400; strong earnings and retail sales boost.
data.description: On Thursday, U.S. stocks saw an increase, as the S&P 500 approached a record high of 6,300. This rise was supported by positive earnings and strong retail sales data, boosting investor confidence. The Nasdaq 100 reached a new high above 23,000, with a 0.9% increase, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average also rose by 0.4% to reach 44,400. Small-cap stocks performed well, with the Russell 2000 advancing by 1.3%. June saw a 0.6% increase in retail sales compared to the revised 0.9% decline in May, surpassing the expected 0.1% gain. Initial jobless claims decreased to 221,000, the lowest since April, indicating a robust labor market. Market concerns eased after President Donald Trump denied reports of considering the removal of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. The tech sector led gains, with the total market capitalization of the top seven stocks reaching $18.4 trillion. However, there were ongoing concerns in the bond market, with 30-year Treasury yields showing some volatility.

data.title: The Heavy Duty Trucks market valued at USD 199.7 billion in 2025, expected to reach USD 342.4 billion in 2034 with 6.2% CAGR.
data.description: In Dublin, a report on the Heavy Duty Trucks Market has been released, now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market, valued at USD 199.7 billion in 2025, is projected to see significant growth with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.2%. By 2034, global sales are estimated to reach USD 342.4 billion. The report analyzes market trends based on truck type (Rigid, Articulated), propulsion type (IC Engine, Electric, FCEV), and application. The Heavy Duty Trucks market is a crucial component of global logistics.

data.title: Rivian sets up East Coast HQ in Atlanta, to open in late 2025 and expand in 2026, part of their investment in Georgia.
data.description: An announcement was made today by Rivian, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, and Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens stating that the American electric vehicle company will be establishing a new East Coast Headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia as part of a significant investment in the state. The office is set to open by late 2025, with plans for additional expansion in 2026 as construction progresses at Rivian's new manufacturing location in Social Circle, located just outside the city. Rivian will be taking up the top floor and lobby of Portman.

data.title: BTS Bioenergy opens new Maryland organics recovery center to meet demand for food waste recycling, supporting circular economy.
data.description: Maryland's circular economy infrastructure receives a boost with the opening of a new facility in Annapolis on July 17, 2025. The facility, established by BTS Bioenergy, a prominent company in organic waste conversion and renewable energy infrastructure, aims to meet the increasing demand for food waste recycling. This development signifies a significant step towards sustainability, with the facility set to handle a substantial amount of organic waste to contribute to the state's environmental goals.
